title: U-Th-K radiolysis-driven deep natural hydrogen generation: Revell Batholith and Athabasca Basin basement analog study

normalized_entities.comparison_aoi: Athabasca Basin crystalline basement, Saskatchewan-Alberta, Canada

key_findings.finding: Revell Batholith is a low-porosity, low-permeability benchmark for radiolytic H2 and sulfate source-term modeling, not an economic natural hydrogen resource benchmark.
key_findings.evidence: Higgins et al. 2025 estimate most probable H2 production at 1.6 nmol m-3 rock yr-1; Revell sulfate production is 10^2-10^6 lower than Kidd Creek.

key_findings.finding: Athabasca Basin basement may host local radiolytic H2 hotspots around U-rich brines, radiogenic felsic intrusives, pegmatites, uraninite/monazite-bearing halos and connected microfractures.
key_findings.evidence: Athabasca brine inclusions contain 0.6-26.8 ppm U in barren basin samples; eastern Athabasca felsic intrusives mostly show 1.6-3.5 microW m-3 heat production with pegmatitic elevations.

key_findings.finding: H2 accumulation potential is governed by a complete source-interface-residence-retention-sink system, not U-Th-K abundance alone.
key_findings.evidence: Radiolysis equations, Revell low-rate result, fracture-width sensitivity from Dzaugis et al. 2016, and sulfate coupling from Li et al. 2016.
key_findings.confidence: high for framework, medium for site-specific prediction

proposed_innovations: H2-sulfate redox balance number B_RS = 4 P_SO4 / P_H2
Radiolytic uranium mobility feedback / radiolytic redox halo hypothesis
critical_equations: d(phi C_H2)/dt = diffusion - advection + radiolysis source - microbial/abiotic/leakage sinks
main_limitations: Higgins et al. 2025 supplementary Monte Carlo input distributions were not extracted in this run.
Revell borehole-level U-Th-K, porosity, fracture aperture, sulfide and groundwater datasets were not parsed.
Athabasca basement fracture-water H2 data were not compiled.
GeoMine MCP CDoGS and Canada geodata tools provided source-planning outputs only; live spatial filtering was not implemented.
